creator: hselasky@c2i.net
text:
On Monday 09 March 2009, Steve Kargl wrote:
> On Mon, Mar 09, 2009 at 12:27:34PM +0100, Hans Petter Selasky wrote:
> > Hi,
> >
> > The patch looks like a compile fix only ...
> >
> > Some thoughts.
> >
> > 1) LibUSB0.1 should really not need to be installed any more on FreeBSD-8
> > current except for the CPP part which I think is not used?
> >
> > 2) LibUSB0.1 port can check if libusb20 is present and install the
> > documentation, if any. Also the LibUSB0.1 port can compile the libusb0.1
> > tests.
> >
> >
> > Here are some patches which do not work 100% because I'm not a autoconf
> > expert, but it might give some clues:
> >

You need to modify the patch before it works. Basically:

1) remove or zero all C-files defining functions that are defined by libusb20
2) remove or zero bsd.c
3) link libusb0.1.12 with libusb20
4) compile libusb

--HPS
